zh.lifeSummary: 當William De Colpton III於大約1250年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可維其哈布盧可時，他的父親Walter de Clopton II42歲，母親Frances Trussell26歲。他於1280年與Elizabeth de Pygott在英國英格蘭素福爾可維其哈布盧可結婚。他們至少是8個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1326年在他的家鄉享年78歲。

nameMeanings.name: Colpton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places throughout England (but especially in the southwest) named Compton, from Old English cumb ‘short, straight valley’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Cumpton
meanings: English (Herefordshire and Worcestershire): variant of   Compton  .

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When William De Colpton III was born about 1250, in Wickhambrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Walter de Clopton II, was 42 and his mother, Frances Trussell, was 26. He married Elizabeth de Pygott in 1280, in Wickhambrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons. He died about 1326, in his hometown, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William De Colpton III est né environ 1250, à Wickhambrook, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Walter de Clopton II, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Frances Trussell, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Pygott en 1280, à Wickhambrook, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils. Il est décédé environ 1326, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 78 ans.

Er hat 1280 in Wickhambrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth de Pygott geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne. Er starb um 1326 im Alter von 78 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

ja.lifeSummary: William De Colpton IIIが約1250年にイギリスイギリススフォルクウィックハンブルークで生まれた時，父Walter de Clopton IIは42歳，母Frances Trussellは26歳であった。1280年にイギリスイギリススフォルクウィックハンブルークでElizabeth de Pygottと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子の親であった。彼は約1326年に故郷で78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William De Colpton III(이)가 대략 1250년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 윅함브룩에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de Clopton II(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Frances Trussell(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1280년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 윅함브룩에서 Elizabeth de Pygott(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1326년 그의 고향에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William De Colpton III родился около 1250 г. в местности виккхамбрук, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Walter de Clopton II было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Frances Trussell, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Pygott в 1280 г. в местности виккхамбрук, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей. Он умер около 1326 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 78 лет.
